mission.title: Install and Configure Devfile Registry on Kubernetes
mission.description: Devfile provides a Kube-native API for cloud development workspaces. The Devfile Registry hosts reusable devfile stacks that define development environments. This mission deploys a self-hosted Devfile Registry on Kubernetes using the official Helm chart.

steps.title: Clone the registry-support repository
steps.description:
Clone the devfile registry-support repository which contains the Helm chart for deploying a Devfile Registry.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/devfile/registry-support.git
cd registry-support
```

steps.title: Install the Devfile Registry using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy the Devfile Registry using the included Helm chart.
```bash
helm install devfile-registry deploy/chart/devfile-registry --namespace devfile --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Devfile Registry pods are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace devfile
kubectl get svc --namespace devfile
```

steps.title: Test the registry API
steps.description:
Verify the registry is serving devfile stacks by querying its API.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/devfile-registry 8080:8080 --namespace devfile &
curl http://localhost:8080/v2index
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Devfile Registry pods running in the devfile namespace. The registry serves devfile stacks via its REST API, allowing developers to discover and use pre-built development environment configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Helm release
uninstall.description: Remove the devfile-registry Helm release from the devfile namespace.

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description: Remove the devfile namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Pull latest registry-support
upgrade.description: Update the registry-support repository to get the latest Helm chart.

upgrade.title: Run helm upgrade
upgrade.description: Upgrade the Devfile Registry deployment with the updated chart.

troubleshooting.title: Registry pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check pod logs for errors and ensure container images can be pulled.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=devfile-registry --namespace devfile
```

troubleshooting.title: Registry returns empty index
troubleshooting.description: Verify the registry was built with devfile stacks included. Check the registry-support build documentation for how to include custom stacks.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm, kubectl, and git installed.
